description_long: This house features a sprawling, multi-wing layout with a complex arrangement of intersecting hip roofs clad in red tiles. The exterior walls are finished in a light, textured material, likely stucco or concrete, providing a strong contrast to the dark, richly colored roof. Several roof sections are at varying heights, creating a dynamic and layered silhouette. The structure includes multiple protruding sections and alcoves, with a central area that appears to serve as the main entrance, marked by a covered porch and a prominent overhang. Windows are distributed asymmetrically, with some grouped in pairs and others as single units, and there are visible wooden or metal slats suggesting balcony or patio areas. The overall design suggests a modern Mediterranean or contemporary ranch style, suitable for a suburban or upscale urban neighborhood.
description: A large, multi-wing house with red-tiled hip roofs, light stucco walls, and a complex, layered silhouette. Features include asymmetrical windows, covered entryways, and several protruding sections.
description_view_0: Front-facing view showing the main entryway with a covered porch, multiple intersecting rooflines, and a prominent central section.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended wings, angular rooflines, and several bay-like protrusions along the facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a large patio or balcony area with slatted coverings and a more open arrangement of windows and roof sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the depth of the structure, additional roof intersections, and the layered arrangement of the house's wings.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered main entry with a prominent overhang, steps, and possible columns or decorative supports.
